reason:
Both answers reach the same correct conclusion (fix CI/CD, not architecture), cite the same Shopify precedent, and give nearly identical remediation steps (merge queue, faster CI, feature flags, automated rollback). The core substance is a tie.

The one meaningful difference: B is more precise about when extraction is ever justified — "Extract a service only when a component has clearly different scaling or team-ownership needs — not to fix deploys" — which directly addresses the key point about real team/scaling seams. A's equivalent line ("If coupling itself becomes painful later, go modular monolith") is vaguer and conflates the modular-monolith trigger with the extraction trigger. B also names concrete tooling (GitHub merge queue, Mergify) and a concrete stat (~40x/day) that make the "continuous deploy" recommendation slightly more actionable, though this is a minor edge rather than decisive.

Neither has factual errors; both are appropriately concise. B's slightly sharper articulation of the extraction criterion gives it a small edge in usefulness.
